sound_type: Simultaneous detonations -- orca breach + multi-species bird colony full alarm
era: Summer. Breeding season peak. Coastal cliff. Maximum annual acoustic event for the combined ecosystem.
ecosystem: Coastal cliff, Pacific Northwest (Protection Island, Tatoosh Island, Destruction Island type locations)
season: Summer (breeding season peak, maximum colony population, maximum transient orca coastal presence)
family: Multi-species: Orcinus orca (Delphinidae) + Uria aalge (Alcidae) + Larus glaucescens (Laridae) + Fratercula cirrhata (Alcidae) + Phalacrocorax pelagicus (Phalacrocoracidae)

theory_nodes.concept: The multiplicative event: orca breach (5,000-6,000 kg adult male, full body breach 80-100% body length clearing water, concussive entry impact audible 1 km+ in calm conditions, water-column pressure wave propagating simultaneously) triggering multi-species colonial nesting cliff alarm (common murre Uria aalge at 20/sq meter density, glaucous-winged gull long-call, tufted puffin, pigeon guillemot, pelagic cormorant wing-thunder on mass takeoff). Breach triggers colony. Colony fills the space the breach opened. The whole exceeds the sum of parts because one event causes the other. Orca breach alone = E=8. Colony alarm alone = E=8. Simultaneous, causal convergence = E=10.
theory_nodes.description: Multiplicative event -- breach triggers colony, E=10 through causal convergence, not addition.

theory_nodes.concept: Colonial seabird alarm wave mechanics: alarm propagates up the cliff face in tiers, each tier responding to original stimulus AND the response of the tier below. Result: layered full-spectrum acoustic event. Murre screeches mid-upper range, gull long-calls upper range, cormorant grunts lower range, guillemot whistles highest register, broadband wing-sound floor from thousands of simultaneous wingbeats. Three to four species, four frequency registers, simultaneous.
theory_nodes.description: Colony alarm wave -- tiered propagation, four species, four frequency registers.

theory_nodes.concept: Summer ecology convergence: colonial seabird populations at annual maximum (breeding season), forage fish (Pacific sand lance, herring) concentrating both birds and marine mammals near same coastal systems, transient orca hunting range intersects cliff haul-out sites (harbor seal, Steller sea lion), atmospheric stability maximizing acoustic propagation. Four independent factors all peaking simultaneously in summer = maximum probability of convergence event.
theory_nodes.description: Summer convergence -- four independent maximum factors.

theory_nodes.concept: Orca breach purposes (documented): social interaction during pod gatherings, long-range communication (surface slap audible to separated pods), play behavior, parasite removal. In transient context near coastal cliff: possibly prey herding or disorientation behavior near seal/sea lion haul-outs. Purpose not fully resolved but the structural intentionality is documented -- breach rates are not random, they increase in specific social and behavioral contexts.
theory_nodes.description: Orca breach purposes -- structural intentionality documented.

theory_nodes.concept: SPS final six acoustic arc: 354 (Breach+Colony, exterior maximum, coastal cliff) → 355 (Stonefly Emergence, inland river surface) → 356 (Pacific Wren Storm Chorus, most intimate E=10, smallest source) → 357 (Orca Female Hunt, coordinated matriarchal) → 358 (All-Species Dawn Chorus, full biodiversity maximum) → 359 (Puget Sound in Silence, held breath, E=10 through weight not volume). Arc from largest exterior event to silence. De-escalation of scale arriving at maximum awareness.
theory_nodes.description: SPS final six -- acoustic arc from exterior maximum to interior silence.

cross_references.relationship: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Simultaneous detonations, multiplicative structure. Breach opens upper register, colony fills lower. Blood Brothers: Whitney opens upper, Votolato fills lower. Same architecture, two domains.

Same species (Orcinus orca), two routes to E=10. 348 = entire community greeting ceremony (~73 individuals, three dialects). 354 = single individual breach triggering cliff colony chaos. Community maximum vs. individual maximum with environmental amplification.
Next SPS entry. Shift from coastal cliff to inland river. From detonation to emergence. From two species converging to one order covering a river surface.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. FIRST E=20 OF THE SPS FINAL SIX. Simultaneous detonations with structural intention. Breach triggers colony; Blood Brothers' dual vocals trigger each other. Both: controlled chaos that exceeds what either component alone could generate.
